# APCP — Construction Project Control Platform

Axion Imagineering's internal project-control app. React (Vite) SPA + Supabase (Postgres + Auth), no custom backend/Edge Functions.

## Core conventions

**Project is root.** Every operational record is scoped by `project_code` (e.g. `SCB`), read from `activeProject` via `useProject()`. Always use `activeProject.project_code` / `activeProject.project_name` — **not** `.code` / `.name` (see Known Issues below).

**Numbering.** Document numbers are generated by `genDocNumber(typeKey, projectCode, sequence)` in `src/config/docTypes.js` — format `TYPE-PROJCODE-YEAR-SEQUENCE`. Don't hand-roll numbering elsewhere. **Exception: MAC (IF05)** uses its own `genMacNumber(projectNumber, sequence)` (also in `docTypes.js`) — format `AI-<project number>-MAC-XXX` (e.g. `AI-0632-MAC-001`), no year. "AI" is a fixed company code; `<project number>` is the trailing segment of the project's own `project_number` field (e.g. `AX-2026-0632` → `0632`), not the project_code abbreviation. Went through two iterations same day (2026-07-07, user-specified, MAC-only): first `MAC-AI-XXX`, then this current `AI-<num>-MAC-XXX` — if you see `MAC-AI-XXX` anywhere (old records, seed data), that's the earlier format, not a bug. The `if05` table's unique constraint had to be changed from a plain `UNIQUE(if05_number)` to a composite `UNIQUE(project_code, if05_number)` to allow the same trailing sequence to legitimately repeat across two different projects.

**Services own data access.** Each domain has a `src/services/xService.js` with plain async methods (`list`, `get`, `create`, `update`) wrapping a single Supabase table — see `supplierService.js` for the canonical shape. Pages call services; they don't inline raw Supabase queries for CRUD.

**"Register" pattern (RFI, Shop Drawing, IR).** These are read-only trackers, auto-populated from their corresponding IF-form — never a separate manually-entered log:

| Register | Source form | Table |
|---|---|---|
| RFI Register (`/rfi-register`) | IF08 — Request For Information (`/rfi`) | `if08` |
| Shop Drawing Register (`/sd-register`) | IF04 — Shop Drawing Submittal (`/shop-drawings`) | `if04` |
| IR Register (`/ir-register`) | IF09 — Activity Inspection Request (`/ir`) | `if09` |

Each register's page component (`src/pages/{rfi,sd,ir}/*Register.jsx`) has **no** create/edit/delete UI — it reads the IF-table directly, derives a display status client-side (never stored), and renders + exports its own A3-landscape PDF. Status derivation:
- Shop Drawing: from the form's existing `response_code` (`A —`/`B —`/`C —`/`D —` prefix) + `status`, mapped to `Pending / Under Review / Approved / Approved with Comments / Revised and Resubmit / Rejected`.
- RFI: from `required_response_date` vs `response_date` → `Under Review / Replied On-Time / Replied Late / Overdue / Cancelled`. No fixed turnaround constant — each RFI's own required date decides it.
- IR: from `result` (`Passed/Conditional Pass/Failed`) mapped onto the same A/B/C/D/UR scheme as Shop Drawing, plus a separate computed `Overdue` flag (fixed 7-day turnaround from `date`/`requested_inspection_date`, since IF09 and IF04 have no per-record required-date field). Overdue is layered on top of "Under Review," never a 6th lettered code.

If you build a new register for another IF-form, follow this exact pattern — read-only, status derived not stored, same print/header convention below. **Exception: MAC (IF05) — see below**, it deliberately does not follow this "separate page" pattern.

Each register's status map and `compute*Status()` function are **exported** (`RFI_STATUS`/`computeRfiStatus` from `RFIRegister.jsx`, `SD_STATUS`/`computeSdStatus`/`isSdOverdue` from `SDRegister.jsx`, `IR_STATUS`/`computeIrStatus`/`isIrOverdue` from `IRRegister.jsx`) specifically so other pages (e.g. `Dashboard.jsx`) can reuse the exact same derivation logic instead of re-implementing it. Do the same for any new register.

**MAC (IF05) is the one exception to the Register pattern above — no separate register page.** There used to be a `MARRegister.jsx` at `/mar` duplicating the IF05 (MAC) data as a read-only tracker, same as RFI/SD/IR. Removed 2026-07-07 — the user judged MAR and MAC to be "the same" (MAC's own list page already showed every record + status), so the register-only page was redundant. Its two capabilities that the plain MAC list didn't already cover — the computed approval status (from `response_code`+`status`) and the bulk A3 register-style PDF export (all MACs on one sheet, summary + legend) — were folded directly into `src/pages/if05/IF05List.jsx` instead of staying a separate route: `MAC_APPROVAL_STATUS` / `computeMacApprovalStatus()` (exported, same reuse convention as the other registers) and an "Export Register (PDF)" button next to "New MAC". The per-record MAC certificate print (`buildIF05()` in `printEngine.js`) is unchanged. Dashboard's Register Summary strip now sources its MAC card from `IF05List.jsx` instead of a `MARRegister.jsx` that no longer exists.

**MAC (IF05) is also deliberately decoupled from MRF (2026-07-07).** It used to have a "Linked MRF" dropdown + `useActivityFill`/`useMRFList` hooks that auto-populated Material/Spec/Brand/Grade/Code/Sample/Activity fields from a selected MRF record — removed entirely per explicit user request, along with the `mrf_number` column's UI (field, table column, search). Activity ID autofill (a separate mechanism, keyed off Activity ID rather than MRF) was removed too, at the same request — every field on the MAC form is now always typed manually, nothing auto-populates from anywhere. The `if05.mrf_number` DB column itself was left in place (unused by the app going forward, old rows keep their historical value) — only the UI/hooks were removed, not the schema.

**Dashboard (`/`, "My Workspace")** has a "Register Summary" strip that reads live from `if05`/`if08`/`if04`/`if09`/`dars` using the exported compute functions above — no duplicate status logic, no stored counts. The MRF-specific KPI grid below it (Delay Risk, Site Availability) is unrelated legacy content, kept as-is since it's still useful, just not renamed to imply it covers the whole project.

**Print convention (all registers + printEngine.js forms).** A3 landscape, three-party header (Client / Employer — Contractor (Axion, hardcoded logo) — Consultant), each cell showing logo-or-name-fallback via a shared `logoCell()` helper. Below that: Log Info (left) → Register Summary (center) → Status Legend (right), in that exact order. `src/utils/printEngine.js` holds the shared `buildHeader()` / `mergeProjectLogos()` used by IF04–IF12 + DAR; the RFI/SD/IR Register pages (and now `IF05List.jsx`'s bulk register export) have their own near-identical `exportPDF()`/`exportMacRegisterPDF()` since they're a different document shape. Axion's own logo is `AXION_LOGO` from `src/utils/axionLogo.js` — never admin-uploadable, always hardcoded. Client/Consultant logos come from `projects.client_logo` / `projects.consultant_logo` (base64, uploaded via Project Register → Parties & Logos).

**People & Access Management** lives entirely under the existing "Roles & Permissions" sidebar entry (`/roles` → `src/pages/admin/RolesPermissions.jsx`) — deliberately *not* a separate "People & Access" nav section. Six internal tabs: Roles & Permissions (matrix), Employee Register, User Accounts, Project Assignments, Digital Signatures, Cost Allocation (placeholder only). Key architectural decisions:
- **No service_role key / Edge Functions anywhere in this app.** Account creation is self-service: an Admin creates an `employees` row (with email); the employee visits `/signup`, and `userService.signUp()` only succeeds if their email matches an *Active* employees row. New accounts default to Viewer; Admin upgrades the role afterward via User Accounts. Forgotten passwords use Supabase's own `resetPasswordForEmail` — never a custom temp-password/hash scheme.
- **User Accounts extends `profiles`** (1:1 with `auth.users`) rather than a separate `app_users` table — `profiles` already was "a person with app access," so the new fields (`employee_id`, `account_status`, `last_login`, `email`) were added onto it.
- Employees are **never hard-deleted** — `employeeService.deactivate()` only flips `status` to Inactive.
- New services: `employeeService`, `userService`, `roleService`, `permissionService`, `projectAssignmentService`, `signatureService`. Config catalog (role list, permission modules/actions) lives in `src/config/peopleAccessConfig.js`.
- **Digital Signatures are applied to print output (2026-07-12)** — no longer just "prepared but not used." `printEngine.js`'s `getSignatureForName(name)` looks up an Employee Register match (case-insensitive exact, `employeeService.getByFullName`) for whatever preparer name a form shows, fetches their active signature (`signatureService.getForEmployee`), and `buildIF04/05/06/07/09` render it as an image over the signing line — falling back to the original blank line if there's no match or no uploaded signature. Wired into each form's `handlePrint()` (now async). Deliberately **not** applied to IF08 (RFI reply) or IF12 (sub-contractor approval) — those signature lines belong to the consultant/client, not Axion staff. Same change also fixed `f.pe` on IF04/06/07/09, which had never actually been populated (real column is `prepared_by`, same mismatch pattern already fixed for MAC's certificate).

## Single sources of truth (config/docTypes.js)

`config/docTypes.js` is canonical for: `RESPONSE_CODES` (A/B/C/D approval codes), `DEFAULT_DISCIPLINES`/`getDisciplines()`/`saveDisciplines()`, `DRAWING_REVISIONS`, `genDocNumber()`. An architecture audit (2026-07-04) found each of these had 1-2 dead duplicate copies floating around (`models/Status.js`, `constants/documentTypes.js`, `constants/disciplines.js`) — those files now just re-export from `config/docTypes.js` rather than redefining anything, so old imports don't break. If you're about to add a discipline list, response-code list, or number format anywhere, it almost certainly already exists here — check before adding.

**Drawing Register vs Shop Drawing Register are intentionally separate**, not duplicates — confirmed by the same audit. Drawing Register (`drawings` table, `/drawing-register`) is a general manual index of any project drawing (architectural/structural/MEP/as-built), no workflow. Shop Drawing Register (from `if04`, `/sd-register`) tracks the *approval workflow* specifically for contractor-submitted shop drawings. They're disconnected today (an approved shop drawing doesn't auto-appear in the master Drawing Register) — that's a known gap, not scheduled.

**`models/Status.js`** is DAR-specific only (its `STATUS`/`STATUS_FLOW` Draft→Submitted→Reviewed→Returned→Approved→Rejected→Closed lifecycle). Every other module's status vocabulary (A/B/C/D codes, Pass/Fail, etc.) is a genuinely different concept and intentionally does *not* import from here — this was a deliberate decision (2026-07-04), not an oversight. The file used to claim to be a universal "one status engine for every module" with unused transition-rule functions (`canTransition`/`nextStatuses`/`STATUS_TRANSITIONS`) that nothing ever called — those were removed as dead code.

## Known issues / deferred work

- **Three parallel numbering engines still exist**: `config/docTypes.js`'s `genDocNumber()` (format `TYPE-PROJCODE-YEAR-SEQ`, used by 9 files — the dominant one), `utils/docNumber.js`'s `generateDocNumber()` (identical format, used only by MRF), and `models/Numbering.js` + `services/numberingService.js`'s `generateNumber()`/`getNextNumber()` (**different format**, no year — `PROJCODE-TYPE-SEQ` — used by DAR and Suppliers, and architecturally the most robust of the three since it queries Supabase for the actual max sequence rather than trusting `items.length + 1`). **Deliberately left unconsolidated** — the user is providing a reference standard to unify around; don't pick one unilaterally. **MAC (IF05) now has a 4th, standalone format** (`genMacNumber()`, currently `AI-<project number>-MAC-XXX`) added 2026-07-07 — this was an explicit MAC-only request, not the reference standard, so don't read it as a signal to consolidate the other three onto its shape either.
- **`activeProject.code` / `.name` bug — fully fixed (2026-07-04).** All 16 files that had it (the original 4 registers + IF04/05/08/09, plus `DARList`, `MRFList`, `ProcurementList`, `IF06List`, `IF07List`, `IF12List`, `DocumentRegister`, `DrawingRegister`, `MRFForm`, `DeliveryList`, `Dashboard`, `Topbar`) now use `.project_code`/`.project_name` consistently. If you see `activeProject.code` or `.name` anywhere going forward, it's a regression, not a known gap.
- **MAC's Supplier field** (on the IF05 form) uses a `<datalist>` autocomplete against `supplierService.dropdown()` — same soft-reference pattern as elsewhere, not a hard foreign key. Free text is still accepted for suppliers not yet in the Supplier Register.
- **RLS is off** on every table except the 5 People & Access tables + `profiles` (re-enabled 2026-07-03 with policies keyed off a `current_user_role()` SQL helper — Admin/Planner write, broad authenticated read). Every other table still has RLS disabled with `GRANT ALL` to `anon`/`authenticated` — access control there is UI-level only, not enforced by the database. Re-enabling it project-wide was explicitly deferred by the user; raise it again next time a new module touches sensitive data.
- **New Supabase tables default to RLS-enabled with zero policies** on this project (confirmed empirically) — if a fresh `CREATE TABLE` mysteriously rejects every insert with `"new row violates row-level security policy"`, that's why. Either add policies or `ALTER TABLE ... DISABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY` to match the rest of the app's current model.
- A `dist/` build-output folder is tracked in git despite being in `.gitignore` (force-added at some point in the past). Harmless — Vercel rebuilds from source and ignores it — but it's repo clutter if anyone wants to clean it up (`git rm -r --cached dist`).
- `xlsx` (used by `boqImportService.js`) is a real `package.json` dependency and installs fine on a clean `npm install` — if your local `node_modules` is missing it, just run `npm install`, it's not a real project problem.
- `constants/documentTypes.js` appears to be entirely dead code (nothing imports from it) beyond the `RESPONSE_CODES` re-export added during cleanup — candidate for full removal later, left alone for now since it wasn't explicitly in scope.

## Deploy

Vercel project `aicc1/apcp`, production alias `https://apcp-nine.vercel.app`, auto-deploys on push to `main`. Pushes currently go out manually via GitHub Desktop.
